title: cymbopogon tortilis var. goeringii
intro: Cymbopogon tortilis var. goeringii, also known as East African lemongrass, is a variant of the tropical grass species Cymbopogon tortilis. It is valued for its essential oils, which are used in traditional medicine and are known for their antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of East African lemongrass make it beneficial for various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, East African lemongrass is often used to make teas, essential oils, and topical creams, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments.
